methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Frédéric Mariotte that has raised €100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 entities in the nuclear energy sector: 3 publicly listed (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only one same-stage comparable and one adjacent-stage comparable, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for robust median analysis. Filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded insufficient overlap. An all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in capital requirements, commercialization timelines, and technology risk across the broader nuclear landscape.

The limited narrow comp set reflects structural challenges in valuing early-stage nuclear ventures: the sector remains sparsely populated at Series A, with meaningful concentration in later-stage and public vehicles. Without a sufficiently dense peer cohort, traditional multiples-based triangulation cannot be reliably applied. A comprehensive valuation would require supplementary methods—DCF analysis of cash burn and path to commercialization, precedent transaction review if available, or staged-financing benchmarks—none of which are present in the current dataset. The €100M raised provides a floor reference point, but deriving a post-money valuation range requires additional data not available here.
